records.ety: [OF. desparagier, F. dparager, to marry unequally; pref. des- (L. dis-) + F. parage extraction, lineage, from L. par equal, peer. See Peer.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Disparaged (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Disparaging (?).]

defs: To match unequally; to degrade or dishonor by an unequal marriage.
quotes: Alas! that any of my nation Should ever so foul disparaged be.

defs: To dishonor by a comparison with what is inferior; to lower in rank or estimation by actions or words; to speak slightingly of; to depreciate; to undervalue.
quotes: Those forbidding appearances which sometimes disparage the actions of men sincerely pious.
Thou durst not thus disparage glorious arms.

defs: Inequality in marriage; marriage with an inferior.
quotes: Dissuaded her from such a disparage.
